title: ADRD Driver Diagram
text:
ADRD Community-Clinical Linkages Framework — Key Driver Diagram




  Draft · Alzheimer's disease & related dementias · community-clinical linkages framework
  Key Driver Diagram — v12


  
    Aim statement
    Adults beginning in midlife are connected with a brain health cascade of care—risk identification, risk reduction, screening, early detection, follow-up, and action—supported by public health's role in embedding brain health across existing prevention and care systems and stewarding community–clinical linkages.
  
  
    6Primary drivers
    
    20Secondary drivers
    
    40Core activities
    
    46Supporting activities
  
  
    Key:
    PD1 — Governance
    PD2 — Population reach
    PD3 — Data & learning
    PD4 — Navigation
    PD5 — Financing
    PD6 — Community
    Supporting activities

title: Julia Chevan
text:
Hi, I am Julia. I am a physical therapist, health services researcher, and public health professional with more than 30 years of experience at the intersection of academia, rehabilitation, implementation science, and chronic disease prevention. 
Currently I serve as Senior Program Manager with the National Association of Chronic Disease Directors. My day-to-day work focuses on building models for community-clinical partnerships that improve outcomes for people living with arthritis, fall risk, and other chronic conditions. In July 2026, I will be joining the Clinical Research PhD Program at the University of Jamestown as faculty.
